title: elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens
intro: Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens, commonly known as Silverberry or Wolfberry, is a plant species in the family Elaeagnaceae. This shrub is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect against chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The therapeutic actions of Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties.
benefits: Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens has many benefits, such as providing medicinal properties that can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. The plant's extracts have been shown to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health, lower cholesterol levels, and regulate blood sugar levels, making it a valuable asset for individuals with diabetes. Additionally, the plant's leaves and berries have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, ultimately improving overall quality of life and promoting well-being. By incorporating Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens into one's lifestyle, individuals can experience a range of benefits that enhance their physical and mental health, leading to a more balanced and fulfilling life.
actions: Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens has many therapeutic actions, such as reducing inflammation and relieving pain, which can improve the quality of life for individuals suffering from arthritis, fibromyalgia, or other chronic conditions, allowing them to engage in daily activities with greater ease and comfort. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can help protect against cell damage, promote healthy skin, and support the immune system, resulting in a more resilient and energetic lifestyle. The plant's antimicrobial properties can also aid in wound healing, prevent infections, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma, thereby enhancing overall well-being and daily functioning. By harnessing the therapeutic potential of Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens, individuals can experience improved cardiovascular health, reduced stress levels, and enhanced mental clarity, leading to a more balanced and fulfilling life.
constituents: Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These compounds, including elaeokanin, elaeokanin-7-O-β-D-glucopyranoside, and kaempferol-3-O-β-D-glucopyranoside, can help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as arthritis, reducing joint pain and swelling, and promoting overall well-being. Additionally, the essential oils present in Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens, including limonene and β-pinene, can exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to prevent infections and soothe skin irritations, thereby improving the quality of life for individuals with compromised immune systems. By harnessing the medicinal properties of this plant, researchers can potentially develop new treatments for various diseases, enhancing human health and well-being.
parts: Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, flowers, and roots, which are traditionally used in various herbal remedies. The leaves of this plant are rich in flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers, on the other hand, contain triterpenoids like ursolic acid, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and anticancer activities. The roots of Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens are also a rich source of iridoids, including elaeagenin, which has been found to have antibacterial and antifungal properties.
preparations: Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties. A tea made from the leaves and stems of Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens is used to treat digestive issues, while a tincture is used to relieve respiratory problems, such as congestion and coughs. Infusions of the plant's roots and bark are employed to treat fever and skin conditions. These herbal preparations have been traditionally used to promote overall health and well-being by stimulating the immune system and reducing inflammation.
side_effects: Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its potential to irritate the digestive system. Ingesting large amounts of the plant's leaves or berries can also lead to allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Additionally, using the plant's extracts or oils in high concentrations can cause skin irritation, such as redness, inflammation, and blistering. Improper use of Elaeagnus lanceolata subsp. rubescens can also interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and diabetes medications, potentially leading to adverse health effects.
